checks.name: recorder dry-run emits valid PPMI schema-probe artifact payload
PPMI X4 V3-GSP eligibility policy is recorded as schema metadata
approval record identity is redacted in schema-probe artifact and errors
approval lifecycle is schema-probe-only before recording

evidence.low_n_tail: invalid schema-probe report: valid_subject_count is below minimum 20
evidence.prereg_tail: invalid schema-probe report: schema probe must not write preregistration
evidence.protected_rows_tail: invalid schema-probe report: probe artifact includes protected row dump

checks.name: recorder rejects preregistration, protected rows, and low-N probes

evidence.output_tail: invalid schema-probe report: observed grouping key contains an unfilled placeholder

checks.name: recorder refuses unfilled schema-probe command-template placeholders
real recording requires an approval record

evidence.output_tail: approval record source is not valid JSON

checks.name: recorder input JSON loader errors fail closed

evidence.output_tail: approval record appears to be synthetic or audit-only metadata

checks.name: synthetic approval record cannot unlock schema-probe recording
default schema-probe output directory is gitignored

evidence.outside_tail: refusing to write schema-probe evidence outside .schema_probes/ without --allow-output-outside-ignored-dir

checks.name: recorder refuses non-ignored output path by default

claim: A post-approval schema-probe report can now be recorded as a scrubbed SchemaProbeArtifactEvidence payload in .schema_probes/. The recorder requires approval evidence for real writes, rejects row dumps, preregistration, model starts, low-N probes, and non-ignored output paths, and does not change any T1/T3 result. Malformed approval/tracker input JSON fails closed without a traceback, approval-record local identity is redacted from emitted payloads and errors, synthetic audit-only approval records cannot unlock schema-probe recording, and unfilled schema-probe command-template placeholders are rejected.
